Businesses entering the US must also navigate complex state and local tax obligations that vary widely by jurisdiction. title: Leave Encashment Addition Deleted as CBDT Increased Exemption Ceiling to ₹25 Lakh
summary: The ITAT allowed exemption of the entire leave encashment amount after noting that the revised ₹25 lakh ceiling exceeded the amount received on retirement. The appeal was allowed by applying the enhanced exemption limit. The post Leave Encashment Addition Deleted as CBDT Increased Exemption Ceiling to ₹25 Lakh appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled to delete a leave encashment addition, citing the CBDT's increased exemption ceiling of ₹25 lakh. The decision allowed the full exemption of the amount received on retirement by applying the revised limit.

title: ITAT Deletes TDS Disallowance as Corresponding Income Was Settled Under VSVS
summary: The ITAT Bangalore held that disallowance under Section 40(a)(i) could not survive once the recipient of the income settled the tax dispute under the Vivad se Vishwas Scheme, 2024. The ruling relied on CBDT Circular No.19/2024 and FAQ No.58 granting consequential relief to the deductor. The post ITAT Deletes TDS Disallowance as Corresponding Income Was Settled Under VSVS appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Bangalore ruled that a TDS disallowance under Section 40(a)(i) cannot be sustained if the corresponding income was settled under the Vivad se Vishwas Scheme, 2024. The decision, relying on CBDT guidance, provides consequential relief to the deductor.

title: Only Net Profit Taxable on Unaccounted Sales, No Double Additions: Delhi ITAT
summary: The ITAT held that once profit is estimated on unaccounted sales, separate additions for wages and operational expenditure cannot be made again under section 69C. The ruling treated such additions as double taxation of the same income stream. The post Only Net Profit Taxable on Unaccounted Sales, No Double Additions: Delhi ITAT appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled that only net profit should be taxable on unaccounted sales, preventing double additions for wages and operational expenditure under Section 69C. The decision clarifies principles against taxing the same income stream twice.

title: No Section 68 Addition on Demonetisation Cash Deposits Supported by Business Receipts: ITAT Delhi
summary: The ITAT held that cash deposits during demonetisation could not be treated as unexplained when the assessee had disclosed sales and provided debtor details. The Tribunal ruled that taxing the same receipts again under Section 68 would amount to double taxation. The post No Section 68 Addition on Demonetisation Cash Deposits Supported by Business Receipts: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled against Section 68 additions for cash deposits made during demonetisation, provided the assessee supported them with disclosed sales and debtor details. The Tribunal emphasized that taxing such legitimate business receipts again would constitute double taxation.

title: ITAT Excludes Manufacturing Companies as Comparables as Assessee Was Predominantly a Trader
summary: The Bangalore ITAT upheld exclusion of manufacturing entities from transfer pricing comparables after finding that the assessee’s activities were mainly trading in nature. The Tribunal held that manufacturers and traders differ significantly in functions, assets, and risks. The post ITAT Excludes Manufacturing Companies as Comparables as Assessee Was Predominantly a Trader appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bangalore ITAT upheld the exclusion of manufacturing companies as comparables in a transfer pricing case, affirming that the assessee was predominantly a trader. The tribunal's decision underscored the critical importance of functional comparability, noting significant differences in functions, assets, and risks between manufacturers and traders.

title: Rework Transfer Pricing Addition as Cyber Media Research Was Comparable to Support Services: ITAT Mumbai
summary: Tribunal directed inclusion of Cyber Media Research Limited after finding that market research and consultancy services were comparable to the assessee’s support service activities. Earlier Tribunal rulings supporting comparability were followed. The post Rework Transfer Pricing Addition as Cyber Media Research Was Comparable to Support Services: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai directed the re-computation of a transfer pricing addition by including Cyber Media Research Limited as a comparable. The tribunal found that the company's market research and consultancy services were comparable to the assessee’s support service activities, aligning with previous rulings on comparability.

title: ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment on Interest Receivables as Assessee Factored Working Capital Impact in Pricing - Taxscan
summary: ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment on Interest Receivables as Assessee Factored Working Capital Impact in Pricing Taxscan

title: ITAT Sends Back Case After TPO Ignored Rectification Claim in Segmental Financials
summary: The Tribunal restored issues relating to comparable company margins and working capital adjustment after finding that the assessee’s submissions required verification. The appeal was allowed for statistical purposes. The post ITAT Sends Back Case After TPO Ignored Rectification Claim in Segmental Financials appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) remanded a transfer pricing case back to the lower authorities. The tribunal determined that the Transfer Pricing Officer (TPO) had failed to consider the assessee's rectification claim regarding segmental financials and working capital adjustments.

title: Transfer Pricing: ITAT Delhi Restricts Corporate Guarantee Fee to 0.30%
summary: Delhi ITAT Restricts Corporate Guarantee Fee to 0.30% After Finding Actual Interest Saving Was Only 0.60%; Holds TP Adjustment Must Reflect Real Benefit From Corporate Guarantee. The post Transfer Pricing: ITAT Delhi Restricts Corporate Guarantee Fee to 0.30% appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) restricted the corporate guarantee fee to 0.30% for transfer pricing purposes. The tribunal observed that the actual interest saving attributable to the guarantee was only 0.60%, emphasizing that TP adjustments must reflect the real economic benefit.

title: Special provisions for computing profits and gains under Income Tax Act, 1961

ai_summary: This article details special provisions for calculating profits and gains under India's Income Tax Act, 1961, covering various sectors and types of income. It touches upon diverse topics from presumptive taxation for small businesses to royalty income for non-residents, providing an overview of tax computation rules.

title: Wholesale & Retail Markets Cannot Be Treated Alike for TP Benchmarking: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi deleted transfer pricing adjustments after holding that retail and after-sales businesses could not be compared with wholesale trading to OEMs. The Tribunal ruled that comparables must satisfy functional and market similarity under Rule 10B(2). The post Wholesale & Retail Markets Cannot Be Treated Alike for TP Benchmarking: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) deleted transfer pricing adjustments, ruling that retail and after-sales businesses are not comparable to wholesale trading to OEMs. The tribunal emphasized that comparable companies must demonstrate functional and market similarity as mandated by Rule 10B(2) for accurate benchmarking.

title: ITAT Allows Vivad Se Vishwas Settlement as Wrong Appeal Was Withdrawn by Bona Fide Mistake
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that an inadvertent withdrawal of the penalty appeal instead of the quantum appeal did not defeat settlement under the Vivad Se Vishwas Scheme. The Tribunal set aside the CIT(A) order and treated the appeal as withdrawn. The post ITAT Allows Vivad Se Vishwas Settlement as Wrong Appeal Was Withdrawn by Bona Fide Mistake appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam ruled that an inadvertent withdrawal of a penalty appeal instead of a quantum appeal did not preclude settlement under the Vivad Se Vishwas Scheme. This decision clarifies procedural leniency for taxpayers participating in India's tax dispute resolution program.

summary: Transfer pricing and business restructuring: Ensuring profit follows functions BusinessWorld - BusinessWorld Online

title: The Transfer Pricing Landscape in Malta - KPMG
summary: The Transfer Pricing Landscape in Malta KPMG
ai_summary: This KPMG article provides an overview of the transfer pricing landscape in Malta, detailing the country's current regulations, compliance requirements, and any recent developments. title: Gujarat HC Quashes Section 148 Reopening Due to Change of Opinion on Section 50C Valuation
summary: Gujarat High Court held that reassessment notices issued after scrutiny assessment were invalid because the Assessing Officer had already examined the Section 50C valuation issue during original proceedings. The Court ruled that reopening amounted to a mere change of opinion. The post Gujarat HC Quashes Section 148 Reopening Due to Change of Opinion on Section 50C Valuation appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Gujarat High Court quashed reassessment notices under Section 148, ruling that reopening based on a mere change of opinion regarding Section 50C valuation was invalid. The court emphasized that the Assessing Officer had already examined the property valuation issue during the original scrutiny assessment.

title: Perpetual Losses Cannot Kill a Royalty Deduction: Delhi High Court
summary: The judgment rejected the practice of assigning a nil arms length price merely because a taxpayer reported financial losses. The court emphasized that transfer pricing rules focus on pricing, not profitability. The post Perpetual Losses Cannot Kill a Royalty Deduction: Delhi High Court appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi High Court rejected the practice of assigning a nil arm's length price for royalties simply because the taxpayer reported financial losses. The court affirmed that transfer pricing rules focus on the pricing of transactions, rather than the overall profitability of the taxpayer.

title: ITAT Quashes Section 148 Notice as Reassessment Was Initiated Beyond Six-Year Limitation
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that a reassessment notice issued after expiry of six years for AY 2015-16 was barred by limitation under the first proviso to Section 149(1). The Tribunal ruled that the amended ten-year reopening period could not revive time-barred cases. The post ITAT Quashes Section 148 Notice as Reassessment Was Initiated Beyond Six-Year Limitation appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam ruled that a reassessment notice issued under Section 148 for Assessment Year 2015-16, beyond the six-year limitation period, was invalid. The Tribunal clarified that the subsequent amendment extending the reopening period to ten years could not retrospectively revive time-barred cases.

title: CBDT Circular Cannot Be Used to Mandate 20% Deposit in Every Stay Application: Delhi HC
summary: The Delhi High Court held that tax authorities should ordinarily grant complete stay of disputed demand when the issue is already covered in favour of the assessee by a binding jurisdictional High Court judgment. The post CBDT Circular Cannot Be Used to Mandate 20% Deposit in Every Stay Application: Delhi HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi High Court held that a CBDT circular cannot impose a mandatory 20% deposit requirement for every stay application against a tax demand. The Court stated that tax authorities should generally grant a complete stay where the issue is already covered in the assessee's favor by a binding jurisdictional High Court judgment.

title: Only Solar Days Count for Determining PE Under India-Saudi Arabia DTAA: ITAT Bangalore
summary: Bangalore ITAT ruled that only solar days and not cumulative man-days should be considered while determining the existence of a Permanent Establishment under the India-Saudi Arabia DTAA. Since the assessee’s stay was only 90 days, no PE was held to exist in India. The post Only Solar Days Count for Determining PE Under India-Saudi Arabia DTAA: ITAT Bangalore appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Bangalore ruled that only solar days, not cumulative man-days, should be considered for determining a Permanent Establishment under the India-Saudi Arabia DTAA. In this case, an assessee's stay of 90 days did not constitute a PE in India, impacting profit attribution for cross-border activities.

title: ITAT Mumbai Deletes Tax Addition Since UAE Consultancy Firm Had No PE in India
summary: Mumbai ITAT held that business receipts from an Indian associated enterprise were not taxable in India because the assessee had no Permanent Establishment under the India-UAE DTAA. The Tribunal ruled that mere provision of personnel did not automatically create a PE. The post ITAT Mumbai Deletes Tax Addition Since UAE Consultancy Firm Had No PE in India appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that a UAE consultancy firm's business receipts from an Indian associated enterprise were not taxable in India due to the absence of a Permanent Establishment under the India-UAE DTAA. The Tribunal emphasized that the mere provision of personnel does not automatically create a PE, clarifying PE criteria for service arrangements.

title: Unlocking Operational Transfer Pricing potential - Deloitte
summary: Unlocking Operational Transfer Pricing potential Deloitte
ai_summary: This Deloitte article explores the potential of Operational Transfer Pricing (OTP) for effective TP management and compliance. It highlights strategies for integrating transfer pricing considerations into daily business operations to streamline processes, enhance accuracy, and mitigate risks.

title: AO Cannot Replace DCF Method With NAV Method for Share Valuation under Rule 11UA: ITAT Mumbai
summary: The Tribunal ruled that once an assessee validly opts for the DCF method and submits a qualified valuation report, the Assessing Officer cannot switch to the NAV method. The decision reinforces the statutory valuation options available under Rule 11UA. The post AO Cannot Replace DCF Method With NAV Method for Share Valuation under Rule 11UA: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT ruled that an Assessing Officer cannot unilaterally replace the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method with the Net Asset Value (NAV) method for share valuation under Rule 11UA if the assessee validly opted for DCF. This decision reinforces the statutory valuation options available to taxpayers for general tax purposes.

title: Concessional 22% Corporate Tax Rate Cannot Be Denied for Procedural Lapse: Rajasthan HC
summary: The Rajasthan High Court held that the benefit of Section 115BAA could not be denied when Form 10-IC was filed within the period permitted under CBDT Circulars. The Court ruled that procedural delay could not defeat a clearly exercised tax option. The post Concessional 22% Corporate Tax Rate Cannot Be Denied for Procedural Lapse: Rajasthan HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Rajasthan High Court held that the concessional 22% corporate tax rate under Section 115BAA cannot be denied solely due to a procedural lapse, provided Form 10-IC was filed within the period permitted by CBDT Circulars. The Court emphasized that a procedural delay should not defeat a clearly exercised tax option.

title: DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar
summary: The ITAT Amritsar held that a valuation report by itself cannot justify addition under Section 69 without evidence of extra payment. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no proof of on-money beyond the registered sale deed value. The post DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Amritsar ruled that a Departmental Valuation Officer's (DVO) valuation report alone is insufficient to justify an addition under Section 69 for unexplained investments without additional proof of extra payment. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no evidence of 'on-money' beyond the registered sale deed value.

title: Competitive Corporate Tax Policy Is Essential for European Economic Growth
summary: Debates over tax fairness and anti-avoidance rules have dominated European tax policy circles for over a decade. However, as geoeconomic pressures increase around the world, policymakers are looking for ways to boost European competitiveness and economic growth.
ai_summary: The article discusses the broader debate surrounding corporate tax policy and anti-avoidance rules in Europe, emphasizing the need for competitive tax policies to foster economic growth amidst global geoeconomic pressures. It highlights a shift in focus among European policymakers towards boosting competitiveness rather than solely concentrating on tax fairness debates.

title: ITAT Deletes Section 43CA Addition as DVO Valuation Difference Was Within 10% Safe Harbour Limit
summary: Mumbai ITAT held that no addition under Section 43CA could survive where difference between agreement value and DVO valuation remained within 10% tolerance band. Tribunal ruled that safe harbour benefit applies once DVO valuation substitutes stamp duty value. The post ITAT Deletes Section 43CA Addition as DVO Valuation Difference Was Within 10% Safe Harbour Limit appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT ruled that no addition under Section 43CA could be made where the difference between the agreement value and the DVO valuation for immovable property was within the 10% safe harbour limit. The Tribunal affirmed that the safe harbour benefit applies once the DVO valuation replaces the stamp duty value.

title: Stamp Duty Value Cannot Be Adopted Without DVO Reference When Property Title Is Disputed
summary: Tribunal ruled that objections relating to defective title, encroachments, and legal disputes require proper valuation examination through a DVO reference. The addition under Section 56(2)(x) was therefore restored to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration. The post Stamp Duty Value Cannot Be Adopted Without DVO Reference When Property Title Is Disputed appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal ruled that the stamp duty value of property cannot be adopted without referring the matter to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) when the property title is disputed due to defects or legal issues. Consequently, an addition under Section 56(2)(x) was remitted to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration.

title: AO Must Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Stamp Duty Value Is Disputed: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi ruled that where an assessee disputes the stamp duty valuation under Section 50C, the Assessing Officer should refer the matter to the Valuation Officer. The Tribunal set aside the capital gains addition for fresh determination. The post AO Must Refer Property Valuation to DVO When Stamp Duty Value Is Disputed: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that if an assessee disputes the stamp duty valuation under Section 50C for immovable property, the Assessing Officer must refer the valuation to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO). The Tribunal set aside the capital gains addition, directing a fresh determination based on proper valuation.
